issues.message: Prop 'editor' type listed as 'Editor' but the public exported type name is 'MarkdownEditorInstance' (Editor is re-exported as MarkdownEditorInstance in bundle/types.ts). Should use 'MarkdownEditorInstance' for consistency with the public API.
Prop 'value' type listed as 'Editor | null' but the public exported type name is 'MarkdownEditorInstance'. Should be 'MarkdownEditorInstance | null' to match the public API surface.
Return type listed as 'Editor | null' but the public exported type name is 'MarkdownEditorInstance'. Should be 'MarkdownEditorInstance | null' to match the public API surface.

Description says 'Returns null if no provider is found' which applies to useMarkdownEditorContext, not the Provider itself. The Provider description is otherwise accurate but source_file points to 'bundle/context.ts' — the Provider is just EditorContext.Provider, a standard React context provider, which is correct.

Description says 'Returns null if no provider is found.' More precisely, it returns the context value which defaults to null (the createContext default). If a provider is present but passes null as value, it also returns null. Minor nuance.

Parameter 'props.preset' default listed as "'full'" which is correct (set in useMarkdownEditor.ts line 36: props.preset ?? 'full'). However the description 'A set of plug-in extensions' is vague — it controls which markdown syntax extensions are enabled (zero=none, commonmark=basic, default/yfm/full=progressively more).
Prop 'stickyToolbar' is marked required:true which matches the TypeScript definition (no '?' in ViewProps). However, no default value is documented — users must always provide this prop explicitly.
